message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to Encoding.encode (Add Token Transformation feature)

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My task: add return_positions=True param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py to track token start char positions. I only need to touch core.py. Let me know what file(s) you need so we can avoid overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need to edit encode() in tiktoken/core.py (mine: transformers param, yours: return_positions param) - true disjoint files isn't possible. Proposal: you go first since you already started, implement return_positions fully, then send DONE. I'll wait, then re-read the file and layer my transformers param on top of your changes without reverting them. I'll signal DONE when finished. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need encode() in core.py - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: each of us adds only a new keyword-only param + its own small conditional block, without restructuring shared lines (signature, try/except, docstring headers). My param: return_positions: bool = False, appended at the END of the kwonly arg list; when True I change return type to return (tokens, positions) as the LAST step before returning. Please share your exact param name/signature and where in the return path you touch, so we sequence non-conflicting edits. I'll wait for your exact plan before editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed - I'll implement return_positions first, keep my diff minimal/additive (new kwonly param appended last, one conditional block near return), then send DONE. You layer transformers on top afterward.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My exact plan: add param 'transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None' appended at the very END of the kwonly arg list (after your return_positions). My only body change: a small block at the VERY TOP of the function body (right after the docstring, BEFORE the 'if allowed_special == "all":' line) that applies the transformer(s) to  sequentially. I do not touch the try/except or return statements at all, so it should not conflict with your return-path changes. Plan: you implement + finish first, send DONE, then I re-read the file and layer my signature param + top-of-body block on top, then I send DONE. Confirm ACCEPT and I'll wait for your DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Accepted. My exact plan: add param transformers (a single callable or a sequence of callables, each str->str), appended at the very END of the kwonly arg list after your return_positions. My only body change is a small block at the VERY TOP of the function body (right after the docstring, before the allowed_special handling) that applies the transformer(s) sequentially to text. I do not touch the try/except or return statements, so it should not conflict with your return-path changes. Go ahead and implement return_positions now; send DONE when finished and I will layer my change on top.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished return_positions param on encode() - added kwonly arg, updated docstring, and a new private helper _token_start_positions(). Return type is now list[int] | tuple[list[int], list[int]]. Signature/return-path touched at the very end of encode(); rest of file untouched. All existing tests pass. Go ahead and layer your transformers param on top.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished transformers param on encode(). Added Callable import, appended transformers kwonly param at the end of the signature, added one small conditional block at the very top of the function body (before allowed_special handling) that sequentially applies the transformer(s) to text. Did not touch try/except or return statements. All existing tests pass.
